prompt:
I need a WebhookManager class that handles the full lifecycle of webhook subscriptions with a hub service.

The hub API is:
  POST /subscriptions          body: {callback_url, events, ttl_ms, ...}
                               reply 201: {id, ttl_ms, ...}
  PATCH /subscriptions/:id     body: {ttl_ms?, ...}
                               reply 200: {id, ttl_ms, ...}
  GET   /subscriptions         reply 200: {subscriptions: [{id, ttl_ms, ...}, ...]}
  DELETE /subscriptions/:id    reply 204

The manager must:

1. register(spec): POST to /subscriptions, store the returned id and ttl, then schedule a renewal at 80% of ttl_ms. Returns the hub's response record.

2. On renewal: PATCH /subscriptions/:id (to keep the lease alive), then reschedule the next renewal at 80% of the ttl_ms returned by the PATCH response.

3. reconcile(): GET /subscriptions, compare to local state, then:
   - For any locally-tracked subscription the hub does not have: re-register it (POST again).
   - For any subscription the hub returns that we don't know about: deregister it (DELETE).

4. shutdown(): DELETE every locally-known subscription (revoke-all). Idempotent — a second call must not throw or send duplicate requests.

The scheduler is injectable: the constructor takes a `schedule(delayMs, callback)` function instead of using setTimeout, so tests can advance time without sleeping.

All HTTP errors (non-2xx) must propagate as thrown Errors with the status code in the message. A failed register must not leave a partial subscription in local state.

Implement in webhooksub.ts. Don't touch the test file.
